marketState: bearish — peak heat wave is already fading while storage surplus, weak seasonality, and above-fair-value price create headwinds beyond 1-2 weeks
summary: Front-month NG is trading at $3.279, roughly $0.32 above the current fair-value midpoint of $2.960 and in the upper half of the $2.46–$3.46 band; the fair midpoint dips further to $2.945 in 7 days before recovering slightly, meaning the relative overvaluation widens near-term before compressing. Near-term weather demand is extreme — 1-7D is above the 5-year maximum range at +43.9% vs the 5y norm — but the 24-hour trend has already collapsed -15 Bcf/d and week-2 demand falls sharply to only +11.1% above 5y norms, confirming a heat dome that is breaking. Seasonal probabilities are the clearest bearish signal: the 4-week window has only a 19% historical chance of a higher close (typical move -1.8%), and the YTD outperformance of +3.3 pp vs median suggests the market has already absorbed more upside than usual for this point in the cooling season. Storage at +151 Bcf above the 5y average is a structural bearish anchor, and the weekly storage injection surprised to the bearish side. Managed-money positioning is heavily net short at the 18th 5-year percentile, providing short-squeeze fuel but not a standalone bullish thesis without a catalyst; the futures curve is unavailable, removing one confirmation tool.

longTerm.entry: $3.28 (current level) or fade any bounce to $3.35–$3.40
longTerm.rationale: Seasonality is the highest-priority signal: 4-week probability of a higher close is only 19% with a typical -1.8% move, and seasonal reliability is low but directionally decisive. Storage at +151 Bcf above the 5y average removes the winter-scarcity premium that historically supports late-summer prices. Price is consistently above fair value across all 4 fair-value windows (now through +21D), and the fair midpoint actually dips slightly next week before recovering — the overvaluation widens before it narrows. COT positioning at the 18th percentile is the principal risk to this view, but without a bullish catalyst the short squeeze is slow to develop.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained close above $3.52 (above the current fair-value band top) driven by a new heat event or supply disruption; or storage draws that flip the 5y surplus to a deficit within 4 weeks.

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D weather demand is still above the 5-year maximum range (+43.9% vs 5y), making it premature to short the heat premium. Simultaneously, the 24h trend has collapsed -15 Bcf/d and week-2 demand drops to just +11.1%, so going long into a fading heat dome also carries high mean-reversion risk. The 5m/15m RSIs are near 34 (oversold at the micro level), while the 4h is bullish — a short-term bounce is possible but would likely be a fade, not a trend. The futures curve is missing, removing the key contango/backwardation read for intraweek positioning.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for demand to drop from +44% to below +15% vs 5y (confirming heat-dome fade confirmed by weather models); on that signal, short a failed rally at $3.30–$3.38 with a stop above $3.52 targeting $3.00–$2.90.

keyDrivers: Extreme 1-7D heat demand (+43.9% vs 5y, above 5y max range) — immediate price support but already collapsing -15 Bcf/d
Seasonal headwind: only 19% probability of higher price in 4 weeks, typical 4-week move -1.8% (low reliability but clear direction)
Storage surplus: +151 Bcf above 5y average with a bearish injection surprise — structural cap on upside
Price above fair value: $3.279 vs $2.960 fair midpoint; fair dips further to $2.945 in 7 days before recovering
Managed-money net short at 18th 5y percentile — short-squeeze fuel without a clear catalyst
risks: Extended or re-intensifying heat dome into late July could sustain prices above $3.40 and trigger stop-out at $3.52+
MM short squeeze: at 18th percentile net short, a surprise weather spike could trigger rapid covering and a sharp move to $3.50–$3.70
Futures curve data missing — if front-month backwardation is stronger than expected (next at $3.226 vs front $3.279), rolling shorts carries additional cost
